steps.title: Read what the graph shows for September
steps.content: For September, the four Puerto Rican cities split into two groups: Rincon, Ponce, and Isabela cluster at relatively low rainfall values (each under 8 inches), while San Sebastian sits noticeably higher, above 8 inches.

steps.title: Match a choice to that pattern
steps.content: The correct option must use the September data to capture this split between the three lower cities and the one higher city.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Rincon's average... is greater than... Ponce': misreads the data — Rincon's September rainfall isn't greater than all three other cities; San Sebastian is higher. ✗
  • Choice B — 'about 5 inches... about 10 inches': wrong pairing and wrong values — Rincon and Ponce aren't both around 5, and Isabela isn't around 10 (San Sebastian is the high one). ✗
  • Choice C — 'Rincon, Ponce, and Isabela... below 8 inches, but San Sebastian... greater than 8 inches': correctly reports the three-versus-one split visible in the September data. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Ponce has a similar average... to San Sebastian': false comparison — Ponce and San Sebastian are far apart in September, not similar. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
